mnemonics.title: P-P-P Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember access specifiers order: Public → Protected → Private. Think: 'Please Pass Papers' (Public, Protected, Private) - accessibility decreases from left to right.

mnemonics.title: Inheritance Types - PUB-PRO-PRI
mnemonics.content: In public inheritance: Public→Public, Protected→Protected, Private→Inaccessible. Remember: PUBlic PUBlic, PROtected PROtected, PRIvate INAccessible.

mnemonics.title: Constructor Order - BDF (Base Derived Finally)
mnemonics.content: Base class constructed first, Derived second, then finally main code executes. Think: 'B' comes before 'D' alphabetically.

mnemonics.title: Protected is Between
mnemonics.content: Protected is between public and private in terms of access. Like a middle child - not fully public, not fully private.

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm using classes and inheritance
Clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use - related to access control

acronyms.expansion: LSP - Objects of superclass should be replaceable with subclass objects
acronyms.usage: Related to inheritance and proper use of public inheritance

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a castle with 3 walls: Outer wall (public) - anyone can enter; Middle wall (protected) - only soldiers (derived classes) can pass; Inner wall (private) - only king (class itself) can access.
Public inheritance = You openly show family traits (public). Protected = You hide family traits but children know them. Private = You keep all family secrets (no one outside knows).

analogies.analogy: Think of a house: Public is like the front yard (visible to all); Protected is like the living room (family and guests - derived classes); Private is like the bedroom (only the owner - the class itself).

Public inheritance: You tell everyone 'I am your child' (is-a). Protected inheritance: You tell only family 'I am related' (hidden from outsiders). Private inheritance: You keep the relationship secret (implementation detail).
